epic.body:
## Why

Modernize `c6cac1a26669eddf` via the AWS Transform plan
emitted by [8.3](#) (wave plan `93b5c8e4-0da0-483c-abfb-d561569df0fc`).

## Scope

- **Waves:** 7 (preflight + cutover + validation included)
- **Files in scope:** 18 (skip-tier files excluded)
- **Cycle breaks:** 0
- **Cluster breaks:** 0
- **High-risk waves:** 6
- **Total estimated effort:** 5.1 d

## How

This epic is generated by the FORA migration planner (8.4) from the
`wave-plan.json` artefact emitted by 8.3. Each child story is
deterministically derived from a single TransformWave or WaveBreak
and carries a stable `idempotency_key` so re-runs do not create
duplicates.

## Board gate

The first child story is the preflight wave; downstream Dev work
(Epic 3) is gated on the `request_confirmation` the migration
planner opens on FORA-85 after this epic lands in the sprint.

payload.body:
## Why

Pre-flight: probe tenant credentials, canary MGN reachability, verify secret rotation, register the repo with AWS Migration Hub.

## Prerequisites

_none_

## AWS services touched

- `migrationhub`
- `secretsmanager`

## Files in scope (0)

_none — infra-only wave_

## Gates

- `canary_probe` (Probe tenant credentials + MGN reachability before any transform wave fires. Implementation: customer-cloud-broker probe-signer (FORA-194)., blocking=True)
- `secret_rotate_check` (Verify every per-platform credential is in the AWS Secrets Manager inventory and within its rotation window. Implementation: mcp-servers/secrets (FORA-128)., blocking=True)

## Commands (seam-routed, not executed by this story)

- `migrationhub.MigrationHub.create_application_component` via `customer-cloud-broker/audit` (audit: `aws.migrationhub.create_component`)

## Effort

`0.0` d

payload.body:
## Why

Cutover: flip DNS / routing to point at the migrated AWS footprint. Refuses to flip if any canary-probe fails.

## Prerequisites

_none_

## AWS services touched

- `route53`
- `cloudfront`
- `migrationhub`

## Files in scope (0)

_none — infra-only wave_

## Gates

- `canary_probe` (Canary-probe every cutover candidate route before DNS flip; refuse to flip if any route is unhealthy., blocking=True)
- `audit_completeness_check` (Pre-cutover audit completeness check across every completed wave., blocking=True)

## Commands (seam-routed, not executed by this story)

- `route53.Route53.change_resource_record_sets` via `customer-cloud-broker/dispatch:route53` (audit: `aws.route53.cutover`)

## Effort

`0.2` d

Validation: smoke test + synthetic canary against the cutover footprint. Refuses to close if either fails.

## Prerequisites

_none_

## AWS services touched

- `cloudwatch`
- `synthetics`
- `migrationhub`

## Files in scope (0)

_none — infra-only wave_

## Gates

- `unit_test` (Run the project's smoke test against the cutover footprint., blocking=True)
- `audit_completeness_check` (Post-cutover audit completeness check; ensures every wave emitted its audit events., blocking=True)

## Commands (seam-routed, not executed by this story)

- `cloudwatch.Synthetics.create_canary` via `customer-cloud-broker/audit` (audit: `aws.synthetics.canary_create`)

## Effort

`0.2` d

notes: Idempotency: re-runs on the same plan_sha yield identical idempotency_keys; the Jira adapter dedupes via external_ref.
Board gate: open a request_confirmation on FORA-85 after the Jira adapter confirms the epic + stories landed in the sprint.
